$comment: D-CSUBSET-32BIT-ALU-FORMS (c74, 2026-07-01): a compound assignment on a SUB-INT lvalue used in VALUE/expression position (`total += (x &= m)`, `if ((flags &= ~M))`) must integer-PROMOTE the base op to int (C99 `a OP= b` == `a = (T)((a) OP (b))`) — else it builds a Char/U8-typed BinaryOp that walls at the LIR sub-native ALU gate. c71/c72/c73 promoted the condition/++--/unary/bit-field sites, and the STATEMENT-position compound assign (`x &= y;`) already promoted via lowerCompoundAssign; but a VALUE-position compound assign lowers through a DIFFERENT path (the lowerExpr work-stack driver's finishAssign + the lowerBinary Assign arm) that built the op at the LVALUE type with un-promoted operands. This was the post-c73 re-probe's last 2 U8 errors (instrumented: op#18=And, operands ordinals [6,3]=(U8,I32) -> U8 result) = sqlite's `if( (p->flags &= ~MASK) )` idiom. FIX (cst_to_hir.cpp, BOTH paths): mirror lowerCompoundAssign — compute the base op at commonArithType(lhs,rhs) (i32 for sub-int), coerce both operands, narrow the result back to the lvalue type for the store; a non-arithmetic common (Ptr compound `p += n`) keeps opType==lv.type -> the c41 stride-Gep, unchanged. Corpus: (x&=0x0F)=15 + (y<<=2)=20 + ((z&=0x0F)==0 -> +3) + (w|=2)=3 + ((s+=9)==1 -> +1) => 42. The VALUE of a compound assign is the STORED (truncated) value, so observables are low-byte (signedness-agnostic unsigned u8, or explicit signed char), matching gcc on x86+aarch64 O0/O2. RED-ON-DISABLE: revert the promote -> error[L_UnsupportedLoweringForOpcode] '<deferred>/Add: integer TypeKind ordinal 6/1 ...'. gcc/clang compile it and exit 42. Sibling of char_alu_integer_promotion (c71) / unary_bitnot_neg_promotion (c72) / bitfield_subint_promotion (c73), same anchor family (the LAST sub-int ALU site: value-position compound assign).
